name: MIB-626 raised NAD in COVID-19 acute kidney injury without clinical biomarker benefit
summary: The disease-context MIB-626 trial supports target engagement and short-term safety but is a boundary condition against assuming NAD increases improve inflammation or disease severity.
statement: In hospitalized adults with COVID-19 and acute kidney injury, MIB-626 safely raised blood NAD and NAD metabolites, but serum creatinine, cystatin-C, AKI markers, CRP, IL-6, TNF-alpha, and disease-severity indices did not differ significantly from placebo.
population_or_model: Hospitalized adults with COVID-19 and acute kidney injury
quantitative_note: The trial randomized 42 adults in a 3:2 ratio to MIB-626 1 g twice daily or placebo.
caveats: COVID-19 and acute kidney injury are disease-context evidence, not a healthy-aging or older-adult prevention setting.
The trial does not support AKI, inflammation, or disease-severity benefit.
The authors attributed the lack of efficacy signal partly to slow NAD rise and suggested future parenteral studies.
